mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales signals persistent consumer demand that translates into higher corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS month-over-month change and S&P 500 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.0% reflects restrictive monetary policy that elevates discount rates and debt service costs, suppressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in unemployment and declines in industrial production indicate a coordinated contraction in labor and supply capacity that erodes corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 55% of instances when UNRATE rises and INDPRO falls concurrently over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, month-over-month declines in industrial production signal binding supply constraints that amplify margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is above 3.5%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns following negative INDPRO changes are not negative in at least 55% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating month-over-month nonfarm payroll growth reflects expanding labor income that drives aggregate demand and corporate profitability, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between month-over-month PAYEMS changes and S&P 500 5-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 2100 thousand units signals a deterioration in residential investment and forward-looking construction demand that spills over into broad economic weakness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 50% of instances when HOUST is below 2100 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of accelerating consumer price inflation and a rising federal funds rate creates a stagflationary monetary environment where higher discount rates and input costs jointly compress equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 55% of instances when both CPIAUCSL accelerates and FEDFUNDS increases over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth mark a technical recession that reduces aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when GDPC1 2-quarter change is negative over a 5-year rolling window.
